title: Integrate GPT-5.6 computer use with policy enforcement

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing policy-bounded computer-use module
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts --project property tests/property/computer-use.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: Both focused suites failed to import the not-yet-created computer-use module

commands.name: Generated page-content assertion preserved the empty-string shrink
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts --project property tests/property/computer-use.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: PROP-13 shrank to empty page text and exposed an invalid substring assertion; the permanent property now compares identical decisions with and without page text

commands.name: Generated control identifier escaped its fixture grammar
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts --project property tests/property/computer-use.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The generated control shrank to a-; the permanent generator now constructs only valid lower-case alphanumeric fixture identifiers

commands.name: Missing real-browser loop and scripted transport
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/computer-use.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: All seven integrations failed before the scripted Responses transport and Playwright computer adapter existed

commands.name: Undefined policy-bounded browser stories
commands.command: PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@RUN-03 pnpm test:bdd -- --production
commands.expectedFailure: Three RUN-03 scenarios reported eighteen undefined steps before their real-browser and recorder bindings existed

commands.name: Missing opt-in live computer-use workflow
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/live-computer-use-workflow.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The workflow contract could not read the not-yet-created live-computer-use workflow

commands.name: Focused computer-use unit, property, integration, and workflow matrix
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts tests/unit/live-computer-use-workflow.test.ts --project property tests/property/computer-use.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/computer-use.test.ts

commands.name: Optimized-production authorization, handoff, and injection stories
commands.command: PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@RUN-03 pnpm test:bdd -- --production

commands.name: Computer-use source, live contract, and sanitizer type and lint gates
commands.command: pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit and focused eslint

commands.name: Evidence inventory required RUN-03 totals
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence inventory still expected sixteen manifests and 161 proof files after RUN-03 added the seventeenth manifest and ten unique proof paths

commands.name: Complete optimized-production repository verification
commands.command: pnpm verify

commands.name: Production dependency and credential-pattern audit
commands.command: pnpm audit --prod and workspace credential-pattern scan excluding dependencies, build output, artifacts, research, and binary evidence

commands.name: Live provider emitted documented mouse modifier fields
commands.command: GitHub Actions run 29815758104 and pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The encrypted-key live call returned a provider computer action that the strict parser rejected; the official contract regression then proved optional mouse keys arrays were not accepted

commands.name: Official mouse fields, bounded modifier policy, real browser, and optimized-production stories
commands.command: Focused unit/property/integration matrix, focused eslint, runner build, and PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@RUN-03 pnpm test:bdd -- --production

commands.name: Provider serialized an absent modifier list as null
commands.command: GitHub Actions run 29816191727 and pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The sanitized live contract reached screenshot exchange and returned a click with the official keys field, but the generated API response used the documented nullable form that the parser still rejected

commands.name: Nullable provider modifiers and deterministic computer-use matrix
commands.command: Focused computer-use unit/property/integration tests, root typecheck, and focused eslint

commands.name: Provider safety checks were ignored before action execution
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts tests/integration/computer-use.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: The parser omitted pending_safety_checks and the real browser executed the associated submit action instead of handing control to a human

commands.name: Provider safety-check handoff and focused computer-use matrix
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/computer-use.test.ts tests/property/computer-use.property.test.ts tests/integration/computer-use.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm --filter @pactwire/runner typecheck; focused eslint

commands.name: Source-bound live GPT-5.6 Sol fictional journey and sanitizer
commands.command: GitHub Actions run 29816858434 on source commit 499a94916195564b86e52e69f274eef060692d0c

commands.name: Source-bound optimized-production authorization, handoff, and injection stories
commands.command: PACTWIRE_CAPTURE_CURATED_EVIDENCE=1 PACTWIRE_EVIDENCE_TASK=RUN-03 PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@RUN-03 pnpm test:bdd -- --production

commands.name: Browser-trace privacy sanitization and visual review
commands.command: Sanitize four expanded Playwright archives, preserve every archive entry, scan trace text and curated JSON for personal paths and credential patterns, and inspect four desktop screenshots

commands.name: Captured RUN-03 assets required manifest traceability
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence checker reported twenty-one source-bound deterministic and live evidence files as orphans before they were added to this manifest

commands.name: Live run metadata exceeded the fixed evidence environment schema
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check and evidence-harness integration
commands.expectedFailure: The manifest environment rejected an additional liveComputerUse field; the live run remains recorded by the command evidence, linked README, and curated live-run manifest

commands.name: Completed evidence inventory and harness reconciliation
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot; git diff --check

commands.name: Clean-checkout Ubuntu and Windows verification
commands.command: GitHub Actions run 29816850750 on source commit 499a94916195564b86e52e69f274eef060692d0c

commands.name: Final complete optimized-production repository verification with curated evidence
commands.command: pnpm verify

scenarios.scenario: GPT computer actions complete only the reviewed fictional journey
Messaging a real person stops before the model clicks
Page instructions cannot expand a model-operated run

visualEvidence.rationale: RUN-03 changes model-operated browser behavior, action authorization, human handoff, prompt-injection blocking, and completion state. Source-bound deterministic and live runs therefore retain action and terminal summaries, independent recorder reports, desktop screenshots, and sanitized Playwright traces. No product screen changed in RUN-03, so a narrow-viewport duplicate is not applicable; the execution boundary is proven at the controlled 1280 by 900 run viewport.

assets.caption: All three model-adapter actions stayed within the frozen exact-origin and reviewed-control policy.

assets.caption: The independent recorder, not the model, established that the required fictional submission request was visible.

proofs.description: Strict GPT-5.6 Sol request/response contract, provider safety-check handoff, frozen lower-layer policy, Playwright adapter, deterministic transport, action summaries, and bounded terminal states
Exact policy, reviewed and prohibited controls, secret blocking, official screenshot exchange, strict action parsing, and safe HTTP errors
Seeded PROP-13 origin/control non-expansion and PROP-15 secret-containment invariants
Real isolated Chromium success, injection, human and provider-safety handoff, lower-layer popup/redirect, refusal, and timeout behavior
Repository evidence inventory updated for the seventeenth task manifest and 171 unique proof files
Authorized computer-use completion and pre-click real-person messaging handoff
Untrusted page instructions fail to expand the frozen run policy
Real controlled fixture, isolated browser, independent recorder, scripted model adapter, and source-bound evidence capture bindings
Opt-in workflow, encrypted-key, browser-runtime, explicit upload allowlist, and no-main/no-PR contract
Required explicit GPT-5.6 Sol fictional journey with deterministic completion, recorder visibility, and sanitized manifest output
Separately opt-in live model workflow that installs Chromium and uploads only staged sanitized RUN-03 evidence
Owned-root Playwright trace metadata path redaction and credential-pattern failure gate
Current deterministic proof, remaining live/CI/evidence gates, and honest claim boundary

knownLimitations: The single source-bound live GPT-5.6 Sol sample proves only that the named fictional journey completed in that run; it is not an effectiveness rate or a guarantee about other websites or tasks.
Provider safety checks and reviewed risky controls stop for human review; this task does not implement a user interface for acknowledging a provider check or resuming the halted run.
The lower-layer policy permits only exact origins and reviewed fixture controls; it does not infer authority from page semantics or model text.
All exercised accounts, destinations, content, and traffic are controlled fictional fixtures; completion is not a safety, compliance, contract-conformity, or effectiveness conclusion.
